Lesson 3 Summary Guide
Email List Growth Chart
 List growth will be relative based on your respective niche
 List growth will like start slow, but then it will hit “critical mass”
leading to some strong growth
 All lists will experience attrition through unsubscribes, wrong
email addresses and bounces
 Leverage your list growth with partner lists (i.e. Hey, check out
this mind-blowing FREE report from my buddy, Frank Kern)

-- 1 of 4 --

2
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
List Size and Responsiveness
 “Open rate” refers to the percentage of people you send an
email to that actually OPEN your email
 Your list will be MOST responsive when you first start out
 The responsiveness will naturally drop off as you add more
names to your list
 Your first subscribers will mean a LOT more to you (i.e.
subscriber #100 will be a LOT more important and valuable to
you than subscriber #10,000.

-- 2 of 4 --

3
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
Recency and Responsiveness
 Age of the list or how long someone has been a subscriber is
very important
 The responsiveness of your list (open rates) is at it’s highest
with NEW subscribers
 Don’t stop adding new people to your list, as it will become less
effective over time

-- 3 of 4 --

4
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
Building Fresh Lists with JV Partners
 Co-promote your list with other targeted JV partners to keep
your list as fresh as possible
Example:
o Guru A has a list of 50,000 (B, C, D, E email for Guru A)
 Guru A will have the “fresh” list and will perform the
best
 Put systems in place to ensure you are keeping your list as new
and fresh as possible
 Never let your list get static; must always be experiencing
constant growth
